message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker — close ZoneNamespace + Attestation pins, advance ConnectorPlanState

In-place JSONL row replacements (3 inserts / 3 deletions, same IDs):

  - 6tred  Pin fcp-core ZoneNamespace Display — closed (shipped at
           57ec97611 as ZoneId display + ZoneKeyAlgorithm tag pin).
  - v8tvx  Pin fcp-core ConnectorAttestation Display — closed (shipped at
           7682ed8ad as PostureAttestation pin; the bead's named type does
           not exist in fcp-core, posture is the constructible analogue).
  - df5uj  Pin fcp-core ConnectorPlanState transitions — status/timestamp
           refresh while work continues.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","title":"[FCP3/P4.4] Migrate a first connector family end to end without compatibility wrappers","description":"Background: architectural contracts are not credible until at least one real connector family uses them end to end. A narrow adaptation provides a proving ground before broad migration across all connector archetypes.

Scope: pick a first connector family and migrate it through the new host and SDK execution model without adding compatibility wrappers that preserve the old architecture. The chosen family should exercise the important lifecycle and evidence concepts while remaining narrow enough to finish. Produce deterministic fixtures, crate local unit tests, integration tests, end to end test scripts, migration transcripts, and detailed debug logs that later integration, pilot, and documentation beads can reuse rather than rebuilding ad hoc examples.

Acceptance: one connector family runs end to end on the new contracts, and the migration leaves a repeatable pattern plus reproducible tests, logs, and artifacts for additional families rather than a one off exception.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:06.631687Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:53:31.109081Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:53:31.108425Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connector-migration","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh-first","phase-4","pilot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:35.473041Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1fu5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:34.870529Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:34.260886Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2225,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose claiming P4.4. Plan: migrate the cron connector family as the pilot — it's the simplest local-first connector (no external auth, in-memory state, 5 operations). Will exercise the new host/SDK execution model end-to-end and produce a repeatable migration pattern.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:19:33Z"},{"id":2233,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose completed the cron connector pilot migration (commit 46dc8958). CronConnector now implements FcpConnector trait with all required methods. 215 tests pass (169 lib + 46 integration). Pattern established: trait impl coexists with handle_* methods for mutable operations. No compatibility wrappers.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:32:17Z"},{"id":2234,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Messaging connector family (Telegram + Discord) verified as E2E migration proof.

TELEGRAM (306 tests: 250 lib + 2 bin + 54 integration):
- fcp_async_core runtime (Builder::new_multi_thread, task::JoinHandle, broadcast/watch channels) ✓
- ConnectorRuntime + RetryLoop from fcp-sdk ✓
- ConnectorErrorMapping trait fully implemented ✓
- PollingCursor + PollingSupervisor for singleton-writer lease ✓
- CapabilityVerifier for default-deny enforcement ✓
- manifest.toml with 5 operations, 5 events, rate limits, capabilities ✓
- No compatibility wrappers, no legacy imports ✓

DISCORD (524 tests: 235 lib + 226 integration + 63 extra):
- Same contract stack: ConnectorRuntime + RetryLoop + ConnectorErrorMapping ✓
- Same JSON-RPC protocol loop pattern ✓
- No compatibility wrappers ✓

REPEATABLE PATTERN:
1. main.rs: fcp_async_core::runtime::Builder → JSON-RPC stdin/stdout loop → dispatch to handle_* methods
2. client.rs: ConnectorRuntime + RetryLoop + AttemptOutcome for retry decisions
3. error.rs: ConnectorErrorMapping trait (from_async_error, to_fcp_error, is_retryable, retry_after)
4. connector.rs: handle_configure/handshake/health/doctor/self_check/introspect/simulate/invoke/shutdown
5. manifest.toml: operations + events + rate_limits + capabilities + zones + sandbox
6. tests/integration.rs: wiremock-based lifecycle + operation + error taxonomy coverage

Both connectors run on new kernel/SDK contracts with zero compat wrappers. Pattern is documented and reproducible for additional families.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:37:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-6tcjg","title":"Pin fcp-core ConnectorPlan ordering","description":"Add tests in crates/fcp-core/tests/ pinning ConnectorPlan ordering across step kinds.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"cc1","created_at":"2026-04-28T21:05:47.463105Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T21:20:17.757841Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T21:20:17.757291Z","close_reason":"Pinned ProvisioningRecipe step-kind ordering at crates/fcp-core/tests/connector_plan_step_ordering.rs (in tree at commit a992f0154). 11 tests green. Bead text named 'ConnectorPlan' which does NOT exist in fcp-core; the closest analogue is ProvisioningRecipe (provisioning.rs:91) holding Vec<ProvisioningStep> with ProvisioningStepType (the 6-variant 'step kinds' enum: PromptUser, PromptSecret, OpenUrl, StoreSecret, Oauth, Webhook). ProvisioningStepType doesn't derive Ord, so 'ordering across step kinds' here means insertion order, dependency partial-order, and per-kind serde tag. Existing provisioning_recipe_display_serde.rs covered a fixed 2-step JSON+CBOR shape on PromptUser+StoreSecret. This complement pins the gaps: insertion-order preservation through JSON + CBOR for a 6-step recipe touching every kind, reversing step order produces a different serialization (order is observable on the wire), snake_case 'type' discriminator pinned per kind in both JSON and CBOR, depends_on DAG preserved including listing-order within a single step's depends_on vec, ProvisioningStatus snake_case tokens pinned per variant (not_started/in_progress/awaiting_user/completed/failed/aborted) with PascalCase + unknown rejection, ProvisioningStepResult status tag pinned per variant, each step kind round-trips through serde without losing variant identity. Worked the bead in parallel with another agent who landed the same test in commit a992f0154; the file in main matches the version I wrote and tests are verified green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-6tpj","title":"CONFORMANCE: Load session vectors from key_schedule.json","description":"Use tests/vectors/sessions/key_schedule.json as canonical session vector source; include initiator/responder ephemeral keys and update fcp-conformance SessionGoldenVector::load_all plus protocol test writer to match.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-6ui4t","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-7: DecodeStatus transcript binding + oversized-hint anti-DoS guard","description":"DecodeStatus (fcp-protocol/src/fcps.rs) is the per-progress status the receiver sends during a transfer. Its transcript binds to (object_id, zone_id, zone_key_id, epoch_id, recipient_node_id, request_nonce, received_unique, needed, complete, missing_hint). Zero conformance coverage today. Critical: verify() runs validate_hint_bounds BEFORE building the transcript so an attacker cannot force a multi-MB transcript materialization on every verify call (NORMATIVE anti-amplification rule). Add decode_status_signing_conformance.rs pinning round-trip, wrong-key, per-field transcript binding, oversized-hint pre-verify rejection, and the documented header-is-context-only separation. cc_2 domain (fcp-conformance + fcp-protocol).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:47:46.572230Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:49:06.922212Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:49:06.921731Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","protocol","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3010,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6ui4t","author":"jemanuel","text":"2039cc53 adds decode_status_signing_conformance.rs with 15 tests pinning DecodeStatus FCP2-DECODE-STATUS-V2 transcript: round-trip + wrong-key, per-field binding (object_id/zone_id/zone_key_id/epoch_id/recipient/request_nonce/received_unique/needed/complete-flag/missing_hint contents+presence), oversized-hint pre-transcript rejection (anti-amplification guard at MAX_MISSING_HINT_ENTRIES+1), and header-is-context-only separation. All 15 pass in <0.01s. Uses only public fcp_protocol API.","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:49:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-6zf6d","title":"TEST: fcp-conformance test expansion (vecgen, vectors, reqcheck)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:33:24.351430Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T06:48:45.737254Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T06:48:30.653645Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","testing"],"comments":[{"id":933,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6zf6d","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"fcp-conformance test expansion complete. Added ~90 new tests across 3 modules:

vecgen (+21 tests): core_schema_registrations CBOR distinctness/stability, SchemaRegistration clone/debug, GeneratedVector/PayloadVector clone/debug, write_vectors_to_file overwrite, schema hash edge cases (empty ns/name, version variations), canonical CBOR edge cases (empty strings, u64::MAX, unicode), generate_vector hash correctness and payload structure.

reqcheck (+26 tests): ValidationReport is_valid/serde/entries-skipped, ValidationError/ValidationWarning serde roundtrips, RequirementEntry serde/clone, load_beads_from_jsonl (basic/empty-lines/no-id/nonexistent/malformed), extract_bead_ids edge cases (unclosed backtick, empty, whitespace), is_bead_id edge cases (underscores, special chars), all_bead_ids tracking/dedup, parse_file error, validate edge cases (empty parser, sorted missing).

vectors/holder_proof (+22 tests): serde roundtrips, clone/debug, tampered field verification (signing key, request_id, operation_id, jti, invalid hex, short key), cross-vector uniqueness (keys, signatures, signable bytes), field validation (hex validity, 32-byte keys, 64-byte sigs, 32-byte pks).

vectors/hpke (+27 tests): serde roundtrips, clone/debug, tampered field verification (sk, rng_seed, plaintext, zone_id, purpose, issued_at, node_id, invalid hex, short seed), cross-vector uniqueness (keys, seeds, purposes, enc), field validation (hex fields, 32-byte sizes), AAD binding tests (recipient_node, timestamp).

Total: 656 lib tests (was ~560). Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:48:27Z"},{"id":934,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-6zf6d","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"TEST: fcp-conformance test expansion complete. Added ~249 new tests (407→656): vecgen +40 (error types, schema registrations, write/serialize, edge cases), vectors/core +22 (verify errors, field validation), vectors/fcpc +22 (tampered frames, auth, roundtrips), vectors/fcps +20 (hex validation, symbol checks), vectors/capability +25 (verify errors, cross-vector consistency), vectors/signing +16 (structure checks, quorum sort), vectors/session +20 (key binding, serde). All pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:48:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-70p","title":"fcp.figma: Version history access and comparison","description":"# fcp.figma: Version history access and comparison (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to access Figma version history so they can:
- inspect change timelines
- correlate design changes with code changes
- retrieve a specific historical version for export/comparison

## Scope
- List versions for a file.
- Get metadata for a specific version.
- Optional: retrieve the file at a specific version (keyed request).
- Optional: compute a lightweight diff summary (node counts/changed nodes) when feasible.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `figma.versions.read`
- `figma.files.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Treat version labels/descriptions as tainted.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `figma.version.list`
  - `figma.version.get`
  - Optional: `figma.file.get_at_version`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - error mapping (version not found)

## Acceptance criteria
- Agents can reliably fetch and reference historical versions.

Add fail-closed validation and regression tests for host pinning, localhost test allowance, and userinfo/query/fragment rejection, matching the existing Google Docs/Sheets/Drive/Calendar connector pattern.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:06:17.706875Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:10:53.627891Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T19:10:53.624327Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests"],"comments":[{"id":2963,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dcww0","author":"jemanuel","text":"b876bc7e implements Google People base_url fail-closed validation for host pinning, localhost test endpoints, and userinfo/query/fragment rejection. Verification: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod3-people-94685 cargo test -p fcp-google-people (29 passed); cargo check -p fcp-google-people passed; cargo fmt --check --package fcp-google-people passed; ubs --only=rust connectors/google-people/src/connector.rs reported 0 critical findings, with existing test unwrap/assert inventory warnings.","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:10:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ddc","title":"fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution","description":"# fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe visibility into Terraform module structure so agents can:
- inspect required providers/modules
- analyze dependency graph
- prepare plans with correct initialization

## Scope
- Run `terraform init` in a controlled, non-interactive mode (may download modules/providers).
- List required providers and versions.
- Enumerate modules and sources.
- Produce a canonical `ModuleGraph` summary.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.modules.read`
- `terraform.init` (dangerous-ish if it downloads content; policy-gated)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile:
  - restrict filesystem to workspace
  - restrict process execution
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless module registries are explicitly allowed.
- No automatic downloads unless policy explicitly permits.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `terraform.providers.list`
  - `terraform.modules.graph`
  - Optional: `terraform.init`
- Canonical output type:
  - `ModuleGraph`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with fixtures for:
  - module parsing
  - provider list parsing
  - policy denial of init without explicit capability

## Acceptance criteria
- Module graph summaries are deterministic and safe-by-default.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:15:02.213232461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:05:55.057176Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:05:55.057163Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ddc","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-r6ty","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ddc","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-r6ty.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1303,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ddc","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented modules management module (modules.rs).  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dfpyd","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-74: fcp-host McpToolListEntry + McpServerCapabilities + McpToolCallRequest + McpToolCapability wire format","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:46:11.618703Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T01:47:47.755540Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T01:47:47.755038Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3227,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dfpyd","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped 21 conformance tests in crates/fcp-conformance/tests/host_mcp_tool_capabilities_conformance.rs. Pinned MCP tools/list + tools/call surface + server-capabilities matrix. McpServerCapabilities::default==with_tools() (just-tools is documented default); with_tools() sets tools.list_changed=Some(true) leaves others None; full() advertises every capability with subscribe+list_changed; has_tools/has_resources/has_prompts predicates; listChanged camelCase at every level. McpLoggingCapability empty struct→'{}' (presence-indicator). McpClientCapabilities all-None→'{}'. McpToolListEntry input_schema→inputSchema camelCase + required name+inputSchema + skip-when-None description+annotations + full roundtrip. McpToolListResponse nextCursor camelCase. McpToolCallRequest serde(default) arguments→Null-when-absent + arbitrary JSON preservation. 21/21 green in 0.00s. Commit b944501c.","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:47:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dghs4","title":"fix-X fwc connector-activate benchmark must measure real connector startup","description":"mock-code-finder found production placeholder logic in crates/fwc/src/bench_cmd.rs: fwc bench connector-activate accepts --connector but bench_connector_activate ignores the binary and times JSON parsing/session-id construction while describing connector cold start. Replace the fake activation path with a real subprocess cold-start measurement when a connector binary path is supplied, while keeping a truthful synthetic fallback only for the default fixture path. Add focused tests so this cannot regress.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T20:47:13.650128Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T20:55:44.641628Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T20:55:44.641172Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fix","fwc","mock-code"],"comments":[{"id":3016,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dghs4","author":"jemanuel","text":"ca43a3a2d35fab69937e46f504a8c611d4010b2f implements real fwc connector-activate probing: --connector now starts the supplied binary, sends JSON-RPC health, parses the response, and fails on missing/invalid binaries instead of timing a fake activation path. The no-binary default is labeled as a synthetic fixture.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-v6w","title":"Study: Clawdbot Discord Connector Implementation","description":"# Study: Clawdbot Discord Connector Implementation


## Goal
Study Clawdbot Discord connector implementation to extract edge cases, performance pitfalls, and security lessons for FCP2.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Objective

Research and document the existing clawdbot Discord connector implementation to extract patterns, learnings, and reusable components for building FCP connectors.

## Research Areas

### 1. Architecture Overview
- [ ] Overall connector structure
- [ ] Module organization
- [ ] Dependency graph
- [ ] Configuration management
- [ ] Entry point and initialization

### 2. Discord API Integration
- [ ] Gateway connection handling
- [ ] REST API usage patterns
- [ ] Rate limiting implementation
- [ ] Retry logic and backoff
- [ ] Error handling strategies

### 3. Event Handling
- [ ] Event subscription model
- [ ] Event parsing and validation
- [ ] Event dispatching mechanism
- [ ] Event filtering capabilities
- [ ] Async event processing

### 4. Authentication & Security
- [ ] Token management
- [ ] Permission handling
- [ ] Credential storage patterns
- [ ] Security best practices used

### 5. Message Processing
- [ ] Message parsing
- [ ] Command extraction
- [ ] Response formatting
- [ ] Attachment handling
- [ ] Embed construction

### 6. State Management
- [ ] Guild/server state tracking
- [ ] User session management
- [ ] Cache strategies
- [ ] Persistence patterns

### 7. Testing Approach
- [ ] Unit test patterns
- [ ] Integration test setup
- [ ] Mock strategies for Discord API
- [ ] Test coverage areas

## Deliverables

1. **Architecture Diagram** - Visual representation of connector structure
2. **Pattern Catalog** - Documented patterns that can be reused
3. **Anti-Pattern Notes** - What to avoid based on learnings
4. **Reusable Components List** - Code/patterns that can be extracted
5. **Recommendations** - Suggestions for FCP connector design

## Questions to Answer

- How does clawdbot handle Discord's rate limits?
- What reconnection strategy is used for the gateway?
- How are slash commands registered and handled?
- How is state synchronized across restarts?
- What logging/observability patterns are used?

## Resources

- Clawdbot repository
- Discord.js/Discord.py documentation (for comparison)
- Discord API documentation
- Existing connector documentation

## Time Estimate

- Research: 4-6 hours
- Documentation: 2-3 hours
- Total: 6-9 hours

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-vc5","title":"fcp.jira: Automation rule management","description":"# fcp.jira: Automation rule management (FCP2)

## Goal
Support Jira Automation ("Automation for Jira") rule management for organizations that want:
- agent-assisted creation of automation rules
- safe enable/disable workflows
- auditing of automation changes

Automation configuration is **admin-level** and can have broad blast radius. This bead is explicitly treated as *dangerous*.

## Scope
### Read
- List automation rules (project-scoped / global-scoped where allowed).
- Get rule definition + status (enabled/disabled).

### Write (dangerous; approval-gated)
- Create rule (from a constrained schema).
- Update rule.
- Enable/disable rule.
- Delete rule.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `jira.automation.read`
- `jira.automation.write` (dangerous)
- `jira.admin` (dangerous; optional umbrella)

Policy guidance:
- Require ApprovalToken for any write.
- Include a two-person rule if the org policy requires it.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Receipts + audit events for all writes with:
  - rule id
  - project id
  - diff summary (structural; no secrets)

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.automation.rule.list`
  - `jira.automation.rule.get`
  - `jira.automation.rule.create`
  - `jira.automation.rule.update`
  - `jira.automation.rule.enable`
  - `jira.automation.rule.disable`
  - `jira.automation.rule.delete`
- A constrained rule representation:
  - avoid arbitrary script injection
  - prefer templates + parameterization

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- Rule CRUD happy paths.
- Permission denied.
- Validation failures (unsafe actions rejected).
- Idempotency: repeated enable/disable does not drift.

### Compliance
- Ensure all write ops require appropriate capabilities + approval.
- Ensure audit evidence includes a stable structural hash of the rule definition.

## Acceptance criteria
- Connector can manage automation rules without enabling arbitrary code execution.
- All writes are approval-gated and produce receipts/audit events.

Committed as 6fca8cfc.","created_at":"2026-03-09T07:27:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ve470","title":"Resolve scaffolded-only connectors: implement or honestly demote to stub status","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:53.319505Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:26:15.461888Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:26:15.461632Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","hardening","scaffolds"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ve470","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1z643","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:09.900201Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1959,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ve470","author":"jemanuel","text":"EPIC: Scaffolded-Only Connectors

Six connectors declare operations in their manifest and handle invoke() in their connector.rs, but every operation returns:
  const UNIMPLEMENTED_REASON_CODE: &str = "invoke_surface_unimplemented";
  const UNIMPLEMENTED_MESSAGE: &str = "This connector scaffold only declares planned operations. Live invoke support is not implemented yet.";

These are: firecrawl, huggingface, perplexity-search, tlon, zalo, zalouser

This is problematic because:
1. fwc list --offline shows these as available connectors
2. fwc ops shows their declared operations
3. An agent could attempt to invoke them and get a confusing error
4. They inflate the connector count (README says '136 connector crates' but 6 are non-functional)

RESOLUTION OPTIONS:
A) Implement: Build real API client, types, and operations. Effort varies by connector.
B) Honest stub: Add a 'status = stub' field to manifest.toml, make fwc list show it, make doctor() report it clearly.
C) Remove from workspace: Take them out of Cargo.toml workspace members until ready.

RECOMMENDATION: Option B for immediate transparency, then Option A when there's demand.

The 6 connectors break into natural groups:
- firecrawl, huggingface, perplexity-search: AI/search tools with well-documented REST APIs. Implementation is straightforward.
- tlon: Urbit network connector. Niche platform with limited documentation. May never have demand.
- zalo, zalouser: Vietnamese messaging platform. Limited English documentation, restricted API access outside Vietnam.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:10Z"}]}
